content.thinking: Let me start by exploring the current state of the Kubernetes cluster to understand the existing Ingress resources and configuration.

content.text: I'll start by inspecting the existing Ingress resources and the nginx controller configuration.

input.description: List all Ingress resources across namespaces

input.command: kubectl get ingress -n demo rate-api -o yaml 2>&1
kubectl get ingress -n demo rate-health -o yaml 2>&1

content.content:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"networking.k8s.io/v1","kind":"Ingress","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"rate-api","namespace":"demo"},"spec":{"ingressClassName":"nginx","rules":[{"host":"rate.example.com","http":{"paths":[{"backend":{"service":{"name":"rate-echo","port":{"number":80}}},"path":"/api","pathType":"Prefix"}]}}]}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-01T05:35:47Z"
  generation: 1
  name: rate-api
  namespace: demo
  resourceVersion: "1725919"
  uid: 5861a89a-816b-492c-97b5-f175d82fb3f2
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - host: rate.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - backend:
          service:
            name: rate-echo
            port:
              number: 80
        path: /api
        pathType: Prefix
status:
  loadBalancer: {}
